architecture.name: Grid-MUSIC-CRB Threshold Benchmark Suite
architecture.strategy_label: Subspace pseudo-spectrum estimation with multi-baseline Monte Carlo SNR sweep
architecture.summary: Implement the formalized 11-step Grid-MUSIC pipeline for two-source ULA DoA estimation, compute stochastic CRB per SNR, and benchmark MUSIC against Root-MUSIC, ESPRIT, DML, and SML in a unified Monte Carlo loop over SNR=0:2:30 dB with resolution-threshold extraction.
architecture.rationale: The formal model provides a rank-D covariance structure and noise-subspace orthogonality, making MUSIC the primary interpretable estimator with required pseudo-spectrum output. ULA structure enables Root-MUSIC and ESPRIT as efficient baselines, while DML/SML provide likelihood references. CRB is mandatory for bound-comparison and threshold-gap masking.
architecture.algorithm_structure: Single notebook workflow: parameter setup -> shared data generation -> per-trial covariance and eigenspace -> estimator block (MUSIC/Root-MUSIC/ESPRIT/DML/SML) -> error/resolution bookkeeping -> CRB computation -> aggregate metrics and threshold extraction -> plots and sensitivity sweeps.
architecture.pseudocode:
1. Build grid/manifold using $\Theta_g=\{\theta_g^{(k)}\},\ \theta_g^{(k)}=(\theta_{\min}+(k-1)\Delta\theta)\pi/180$, $a_m(\theta)=e^{-j\pi m\sin\theta}$, and $\mathbf{A}_0=[\mathbf{a}(\theta_{0,1}),\mathbf{a}(\theta_{0,2})]$.
2. For each SNR index $q$ and trial $t$, compute $\gamma_q=10^{\mathrm{SNR}_{\mathrm{dB}}[q]/10}$, set $\sigma_s^2=1$, and $\sigma_n^2=D\sigma_s^2/\gamma_q$.
3. Generate random snapshots $\mathbf{S}\sim\mathcal{CN}(0,\sigma_s^2)$ and $\mathbf{N}\sim\mathcal{CN}(0,\sigma_n^2)$ via $S_{d,\ell}\sim\mathcal{CN}(0,\sigma_s^2)$ and $N_{m,\ell}\sim\mathcal{CN}(0,\sigma_n^2)$.
4. Form received data $\mathbf{X}=\mathbf{A}_0\mathbf{S}+\mathbf{N}$.
5. Estimate covariance $\hat{\mathbf{R}}_x=\frac{1}{L}\mathbf{X}\mathbf{X}^H$.
6. Eigendecompose and sort: $\hat{\mathbf{R}}_x\mathbf{u}_i=\lambda_i\mathbf{u}_i$, $\lambda_1\ge\cdots\ge\lambda_M$, $\mathbf{U}_n=[\mathbf{u}_{D+1},\dots,\mathbf{u}_M]$.
7. MUSIC scan: for each grid point, compute $d_k=\mathbf{a}_k^H\mathbf{U}_n\mathbf{U}_n^H\mathbf{a}_k$ and $P_k=1/\Re\{d_k\}$.
8. Peak localization using $\mathcal{K}_{\mathrm{pk}}=\{k: P_k>P_{k-1}\land P_k\ge P_{k+1}\}$, choose $\hat{k}_{1:2}=\operatorname*{arg\,top2}_{k\in\mathcal{K}_{\mathrm{pk}}}P_k$, return $\hat{\theta}_{1:2}=\operatorname{sort}(\theta_g^{(\hat{k}_{1:2})})$.
9. Per-trial metrics: $\mathbf{e}_t=\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}_t-\boldsymbol{\theta}_0$ and $\mathcal{R}_t=\mathbf{1}\{\text{two dominant peaks and each within }\pm2^\circ\}$.
10. Aggregate by SNR: $\mathrm{RMSE}_q=\sqrt{\frac{1}{DT}\sum_t\|\mathbf{e}_{q,t}\|_2^2}\cdot\frac{180}{\pi}$, $\hat{P}_{\mathrm{res},q}=\frac{1}{T}\sum_t\mathcal{R}_{q,t}$, threshold mask $\mathbf{1}\{\mathrm{RMSE}_q>3\sqrt{\frac{1}{D}\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{CRB}_q)}\frac{180}{\pi}\}$, and $\mathrm{SNR}_{\mathrm{th}}=\min\{\mathrm{SNR}_{\mathrm{dB}}[q]:\hat{P}_{\mathrm{res},q}\ge0.9\}$.
11. CRB per SNR: $\mathbf{R}_x=\mathbf{A}_0\mathbf{R}_s\mathbf{A}_0^H+\sigma_n^2\mathbf{I}$, $[\mathbf{J}_q]_{ij}=2L\Re\{\operatorname{tr}(\mathbf{R}_x^{-1}\frac{\partial\mathbf{R}_x}{\partial\theta_i}\mathbf{R}_x^{-1}\frac{\partial\mathbf{R}_x}{\partial\theta_j})\}$, and $\mathrm{CRB}_q=\mathbf{J}_q^{-1}$; evaluate all baselines in the same loop for fair comparison.
architecture.estimated_complexity: O(Q T (M^3 + G M (M-D) + C_RM + C_ES + C_DML + C_SML)), with C_DML/C_SML dominated by coarse-to-fine 2D angle search
architecture.potential_weakness: DML/SML can be runtime-heavy and may require coarse-to-fine search and capped iterations to remain notebook-feasible with T=1000.

algorithm_spec.objective: Estimate two DoAs and MUSIC pseudo-spectrum under the model $\mathbf{x}[\ell]=\mathbf{A}(\boldsymbol{\theta})\mathbf{s}[\ell]+\mathbf{n}[\ell]$, then compute RMSE/resolution-vs-SNR and compare against stochastic CRB and baseline estimators.

inputs.name: scan_grid_deg
inputs.description: MUSIC scan grid from -90 to 90 with 0.1 degree step.

inputs.name: random_seed
inputs.description: Seed for reproducibility and shared realizations across estimators.

outputs.name: theta_hat_deg
outputs.description: Estimated ordered DoAs for each estimator.

outputs.name: resolution_probability_per_snr
outputs.description: Probability that two-source resolution rule is satisfied.

outputs.name: resolution_threshold_snr_db
outputs.description: Minimum SNR where resolution probability reaches 0.9.

pipeline.name: Build angle grid and true manifold
pipeline.purpose: Create search grid and steering matrix for true DoAs.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use np.arange for grid and np.exp with broadcasting over sensor indices and angles.

pipeline.name: Loop over SNR and trials with power scaling
pipeline.purpose: Set per-SNR noise variance under per-sensor SNR definition.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Convert dB using 10**(snr_db/10.0); keep nested loops for reproducible Monte Carlo.

pipeline.name: Generate source and noise snapshots
pipeline.purpose: Draw random complex Gaussian source/noise matrices.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use rng.standard_normal for real/imag parts, scaled by sqrt(var/2).

pipeline.name: Synthesize received snapshots
pipeline.purpose: Construct observed array data from manifold plus noise.

pipeline.name: Estimate sample covariance
pipeline.purpose: Obtain finite-snapshot covariance for subspace methods.

pipeline.name: Eigendecompose and form noise subspace
pipeline.purpose: Separate signal/noise subspaces given known D=2.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use np.linalg.eigh and descending eigenvalue sort.

pipeline.name: Compute MUSIC pseudo-spectrum
pipeline.implementation_hint: Precompute Agrid and evaluate projector action vectorized.

pipeline.name: Peak picking and DoA estimate
pipeline.purpose: Find two dominant pseudo-spectrum peaks and map to DoAs.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use scipy.signal.find_peaks or local-max boolean mask + argpartition.

pipeline.name: Trial error and resolution indicator
pipeline.purpose: Score estimation error and success under two-peak rule.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Sort true/estimated angles and apply nearest-distance checks in radians.

pipeline.name: Aggregate metrics and extract threshold
pipeline.purpose: Compute RMSE, resolution probability, threshold mask, and SNR threshold.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use np.mean/np.sqrt and np.where for first valid threshold index.

pipeline.name: Compute stochastic CRB per SNR
pipeline.purpose: Generate theoretical benchmark for RMSE and threshold-gap analysis.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use analytical dA/dtheta for ULA and 2x2 FIM inversion.

core_assumptions: Narrowband far-field plane-wave model with ideal calibration.
D=2 is known and fixed for all subspace methods.
Sources are uncorrelated circular complex Gaussian with equal power.
Noise is spatially white circular complex Gaussian.
Per-sensor input SNR scaling is consistent in simulation and CRB.
implementation_constraints: All code runs inside notebook cells using NumPy/SciPy/Matplotlib only.
Use identical random realizations across estimators in each trial for fairness.
DML/SML implemented with practical coarse-to-fine 2D search to control runtime.
Peak detection must enforce exactly-two-dominant-peaks logic for resolution metric.
failure_modes: Merged peaks near threshold SNR causing missed resolution.
Grid quantization bias in MUSIC/DML/SML when grid is too coarse.
Subspace swap at low SNR/small L causing catastrophic angle errors.
Numerical instability in SML covariance inversion when candidate model is ill-conditioned.

primary_metrics.name: RMSE_deg
primary_metrics.description: Main DoA accuracy metric across SNR.

primary_metrics.name: ResolutionProbability
primary_metrics.description: Probability that exactly two dominant peaks are correctly localized within ±2°.

primary_metrics.name: RMSE_to_CRB_ratio
primary_metrics.description: Gap-to-bound indicator; threshold region flagged when ratio > 3.

secondary_metrics.name: resolution_threshold_snr_db
secondary_metrics.description: Minimum SNR where resolution probability reaches target 0.9.

secondary_metrics.name: runtime_per_trial_ms
secondary_metrics.description: Average runtime per trial per estimator.

secondary_metrics.name: pseudospectrum_peak_separation_deg
secondary_metrics.description: Distance between two dominant MUSIC peaks.

baseline_methods.name: Root-MUSIC
baseline_methods.description: ULA polynomial-rooting subspace estimator without dense grid scan.
baseline_methods.implementation_hint: Build polynomial coefficients from projector diagonals, use np.roots, select roots nearest unit circle.

baseline_methods.name: ESPRIT
baseline_methods.implementation_hint: Use np.linalg.pinv and eigenvalues of DxD matrix.

baseline_methods.name: Deterministic Maximum Likelihood
baseline_methods.implementation_hint: Use coarse global 2D grid + local refinement around best pair.

baseline_methods.name: Stochastic Maximum Likelihood
baseline_methods.description: Gaussian covariance likelihood fitting with nuisance covariance parameters.
baseline_methods.implementation_hint: Angle-grid outer loop with closed-form/iterative nuisance updates, capped iterations.

sensitivity_factors.description: Controls covariance estimation quality and subspace leakage.
Directly impacts resolvability and threshold behavior.
Changes aperture and subspace dimensionality, affecting resolution and CRB.

plots.title: DoA RMSE vs SNR
plots.description: Primary performance comparison and asymptotic approach to CRB.

plots.title: Resolution Probability vs SNR (with 0.9 target)
plots.description: Extracts resolution threshold SNR for each method.

plots.title: MUSIC Pseudo-spectrum at 20 dB
plots.description: Shows peak localization behavior and separation around true DoAs.

plots.title: Sensitivity: RMSE vs SNR for multiple snapshot counts
plots.description: Demonstrates finite-snapshot effect on threshold and high-SNR slope.

boundary_conditions.name: High SNR regime (>=24 dB)
boundary_conditions.description: Estimator should become near-efficient under accurate subspace separation.
boundary_conditions.expected_behavior: RMSE approaches CRB and RMSE/CRB ratio tends toward 1 for MUSIC/Root-MUSIC/ESPRIT.

boundary_conditions.name: Low SNR regime (<=6 dB)
boundary_conditions.description: Subspace leakage and peak swaps expected.
boundary_conditions.expected_behavior: RMSE inflation, ratio >3, and reduced resolution probability.

notebook_plan.purpose: State experiment goal, model, and deliverables.
must_include: Problem statement with M=8, D=2, L=200
Metrics: RMSE, resolution probability, CRB ratio

notebook_plan.purpose: Define scenario constants, assumptions, and resolution rule.
Document formal equations used in implementation.
must_include: MUSIC pseudo-spectrum and CRB FIM equations
Step-10 aggregation formulas for RMSE and threshold

notebook_plan.purpose: Load notebook-only dependencies and deterministic RNG setup.
must_include: timing utility and random seed initialization

notebook_plan.purpose: Implement steering-matrix, snapshot simulation, and shared trial generator.
Implement MUSIC (with pseudo-spectrum), Root-MUSIC, ESPRIT, DML, SML, and CRB functions.
Create sweep function that loops across every SNR point and optionally sensitivity factors; computes all metrics for all algorithms in the same loop.
must_include: for snr_db in snr_db_grid loop (no replication shortcuts)
inner Monte Carlo loop over trials
evaluate MUSIC + all baselines on same X
compute RMSE, P_res, runtime, RMSE/CRB ratio, threshold mask
support additional outer loop over sensitivity_factors

notebook_plan.purpose: Run nominal sweep and sensitivity sweeps; collect result dictionaries with required keys.
must_include: Call sweep for L/separation/M sensitivity settings
Store outputs with keys algorithm, elapsed_sec, performance_data
RMSE vs SNR semilogy with CRB overlay
Resolution probability vs SNR with 0.9 line
20 dB MUSIC pseudo-spectrum plot with true-angle markers
At least one sensitivity comparison plot

notebook_plan.purpose: Summarize threshold SNR, CRB-gap region, and baseline ranking.

algorithm_steps.name: Build angle grid and true manifold
Loop over SNR and trials with power scaling

implementation_constraints: All code runs inside notebook cells using NumPy/SciPy/Matplotlib only.
Use identical random realizations across estimators in each trial for fairness.
DML/SML implemented with practical coarse-to-fine 2D search to control runtime.
Peak detection must enforce exactly-two-dominant-peaks logic for resolution metric.
